- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
`high effort → 3 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters
more than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.

## Phase 0 — Gather the diff

Run `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`
if there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are
uncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and
include the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the
commit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,
review that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.

## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 angles, up to 6 each)

Run **3 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each
surfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line
`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.

### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan

Read every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for
each hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR
re-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,
or platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,
off-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.

### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor

For every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it
enforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.
If you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error
path, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.

### Angle C — cross-file tracer

For each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and
check whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed
return shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:
does a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?

Pass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that
silently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the
dominant cause of misses.

## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)

Dedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For
each remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:
give it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly
one of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.

**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being "speculative" or
"depends on runtime state" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,
nil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing
optional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the
code does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that
lost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.

**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the
actual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already
handled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.

Keep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.

## Output

Return findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "path/to/file.ext",
    "line": 123,
    "summary": "one-sentence statement of the bug",
    "failure_scenario": "concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash"
  }
]
```

Ranked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most
severe. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.
Let me gather the diff first.

514	+func startJobsMode(ctx context.Context, eg *errgroup.Group, app *cli.Cli, cfg *sharedConfig) {
515	+	kafkaTopic := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
516	+		Name:   "kafka-topic-out",
517	+		Desc:   "Kafka topic to publish lifecycle events to",
518	+		EnvVar: "KAFKA_TOPIC_OUT",
519	+	})
520	+
521	+	mongoDB, err := tmongo.InitMongoDB(ctx, tmongo.MongoOpts{
522	+		Url:          *cfg.mongoURL,
523	+		DatabaseName: *cfg.mongoDBName,
524	+	})
525	+	if err != nil {
526	+		tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to initialise mongodb")
527	+	}
528	+
529	+	db, err := storage.NewStorage(ctx, *mongoDB, time.Now)
530	+	if err != nil {
531	+		tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "failed to create mongo store")
532	+	}
533	+
534	+	// Kafka producer for lifecycle events
535	+	kafkaSink := tpubsub.InitAsyncSink(ctx, tpubsub.PubSubSinkOpts{
536	+		PubSubOpts: tpubsub.PubSubOpts{
537	+			AppName: appName,
538	+			Brokers: *cfg.kafkaBrokers,
539	+			Topic:   *kafkaTopic,
540	+			Version: *cfg.kafkaVersion,
541	+		},
542	+	})
543	+	defer kafkaSink.Sink.Close()
544	+
545	+	protostrateProd := protostrate.NewProducer(
546	+		protostrate_tools.NewAsyncSubstrateProducerDestination(kafkaSink.Sink),
547	+		protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewSenderDecoratorInterceptor(appName, tvars.Namespace, "")),
548	+		protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(protostrate_instrumented.NewProducerMetrics()),
549	+		protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewTokenCorrelationInterceptor()),
550	+		protostrate.WithProducerInterceptor(producer.NewAuditInterceptor()),
551	+		tpubsub.WithProducerEnvelopeTranslator(),
552	+	)
553	+	defer protostrateProd.Close()
554	+	go func() {
555	+		if err := protostrateProd.Produce(ctx); err != nil {
556	+			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "stopped producing messages")
557	+		}
558	+	}()
559	+
560	+	eventProducer := events.NewProducer(serviceevents.NewServiceeventsProducer(protostrateProd))
561	+
562	+	// Setup scheduler for handset updates
563	+	tlog.Info(ctx, "Setup scheduler for handset updates")
564	+	jobs := jobs.New(ctx, db, eventProducer)
565	+	jobs.Start()
566	+	defer jobs.Stop()
567	+
568	+	opsServer := tbootstrap.InitOpsServer(ctx, tbootstrap.OpsServerOpts{
569	+		AppName:        appName,
570	+		AppDescription: appDesc,
571	+		Revision:       gitHash,
572	+		Addr:           *cfg.opsAddress,
573	+	})
574	+
575	+	err = tbootstrap.StartBootstrapGroup(eg,
576	+		tbootstrap.WithMongoClient(ctx, *mongoDB),
577	+		tbootstrap.WithProtostrateProducer(ctx, protostrateProd, *kafkaTopic),
578	+		tbootstrap.WithOpsServer(ctx, opsServer),
579	+	)
580	+	if err != nil {
581	+		tlog.WithError(err).Error(ctx, "error returned from error group")
582	+		os.Exit(1)
583	+	}
584	+}

851	+package jobs
852	+
853	+import (
854	+	"context"
855	+	"fmt"
856	+
857	+	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
858	+

863	+func expireContractsRunner(store Storage, eventProducer Producer) func(ctx context.Context) {
864	+	return func(ctx context.Context) {
865	+		contracts, err := store.FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts(ctx)
866	+		if err != nil {
867	+			// The function above returns any successful contracts before an error occurred
868	+			tlog.WithError(err).
869	+				WithAttribute("successful_count", fmt.Sprintf("%d", len(contracts))).
870	+				Error(ctx, "failed to find and update expired contracts")
871	+		}
872	+
873	+		for _, contract := range contracts {
874	+			var expiresAt *timestamppb.Timestamp
875	+			if contract.ExpiresAt != nil {
876	+				expiresAt = timestamppb.New(*contract.ExpiresAt)
877	+			}
878	+
879	+			if err := eventProducer.ProduceMobileHandsetContractExpiredSync(ctx, &serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractExpired{
880	+				ServiceId:  contract.ServiceID,
881	+				CustomerId: contract.CustomerID,
882	+				ContractId: contract.ID.String(),
883	+				HandsetId:  contract.HandsetID,
884	+				ExpiresAt:  expiresAt,
885	+			}); err != nil {
886	+				tlog.WithError(err).
887	+					WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
888	+					Error(ctx, "failed to produce MobileHandsetContractExpired event")
889	+			}
890	+		}
891	+	}
892	+}

input.prompt:
You are Angle B for a code review: audit removed/replaced behavior.

## Context
This branch adds contract expiry tracking to a handset service. The diff touches:
- `services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer_test.go` — rewritten from stdlib `testing` to Ginkgo/Gomega
- `services/handset/internal/service/mocks/service.go` — `UpdateContractBankDetails` moved (deleted then re-added higher up)
- `services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go` — `NewStorage` and `NewStorageWithCollections` gain a `nowFunc` parameter

## Key deletions to audit

### 1. consumer_test.go rewrite
The old tests covered:
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedUpdatesContractStatus` — APPROVED → HANDSET_ORDERED, ack called
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedSkipsAlreadyOrderedContract` — HANDSET_ORDERED → ack without status update
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedWaitsForApprovedContract` — non-APPROVED (ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED) → error, no ack
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedSetsExpiresAt` — new test added
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedSkipsExpiresAtWhenSignedAtNil` — new test added
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedReturnsErrorOnInvalidContractMonths` — new test added
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedReturnsErrorWhenExpiresAtUpdateFails` — new test added
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetContractApprovedSkipsOrderedContract` — acked when already HANDSET_ORDERED
- `TestConsumeMobileHandsetContractApprovedUsesApprovedEventLoanValues` — loanbox CreateLoan gets retail price, contract months, bank account

The new Ginkgo file covers `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered` and `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` describe blocks.

**Question**: Does the new Ginkgo `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered` describe block cover the first case — APPROVED contract with no signedAt or contractMonths set, where it should update status and ack? Specifically, check if the "updates contract status and acks" happy path for a plain APPROVED contract (no ExpiresAt logic) is still tested.

Also check: the old test `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedUpdatesContractStatus` passed `ServiceId`, `CustomerId`, `HandsetId` in the event payload. Does the new test pass those? (They may be irrelevant to the handler, but worth noting if the test became less precise.)

### 2. The working-tree diff shows consumer_test.go being converted from the intermediate form (which had the new `testing`-based tests added in a prior commit) to Ginkgo. Check that ALL the test cases that were in the intermediate form survive in the new Ginkgo form.

The intermediate form (from the staged working tree diff) includes:
```
TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedUpdatesContractStatus
TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedSkipsAlreadyOrderedContract
```

I have everything I need. Here are the findings:

---

**1. Happy-path APPROVED contract (no ExpiresAt) — missing status-update assertion**

File: `consumer_test.go`, line 77–95

The Ginkgo case "contract is APPROVED with no signedAt" tests the `SignedAt == nil` branch, but the original `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedUpdatesContractStatus` tested a simpler scenario: APPROVED contract with **neither** `ContractMonths` nor `SignedAt` set (zero values). The new test sets `ContractMonths: "24"` in the nil-signedAt case, which is fine, but crucially — neither the old nor the new test asserts that `UpdateContractStatusByID` is called with `gomock.Any()` strictness on the new status value. Both use exact matching, so this is equivalent. Not a real gap.

However: **the new "no signedAt" case does not test the plain APPROVED contract as originally written** — the old test used an empty `ContractMonths: ""`. An empty `ContractMonths` with a nil `SignedAt` still hits `ack()` (the `SignedAt != nil` guard skips the `Atoi` call), so the empty-string case is vacuously safe. But no new test covers it, so a future regression where `Atoi("")` is called would go undetected. Low severity.

---

**2. `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedUpdatesContractStatus` — event payload fields dropped**

File: `consumer_test.go`, ~line 46–53

The old test passed `ServiceId`, `CustomerId`, `HandsetId` in the `MobileHandsetOrdered` payload. The new Ginkgo `consume` helper passes only `ContractId`. The handler does not currently read those fields, so there is no behavioral gap. But the test's signal value is reduced: if someone adds code that reads `ServiceId` from this event and breaks on empty, no test would catch it.

---

**3. `TestConsumeMobileHandsetContractApprovedSkipsOrderedContract` — idempotency guard is incomplete**

File: `consumer_test.go`, line 208–218

The old test and the new Ginkgo version both only check `HANDSET_ORDERED` triggers the early-ack path. But `consumer.go` line 83 also skips on `ContractStatusApproved` (`contract.Status == models.ContractStatusApproved || ...`). Neither the old tests nor the new Ginkgo tests cover the `APPROVED`-status skip branch. This is a pre-existing gap that survived the rewrite — worth flagging because the second condition is new (added in this branch) and is entirely untested.

---

**4. `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedReturnsErrorOnInvalidContractMonths` — acked assertion weakened**

File: `consumer_test.go`, line 124–143

The old test only checked `err == nil` (negated). The new Ginkgo version adds `Expect(acked).To(BeFalse())`. This is actually **stronger**, not weaker. No gap here.

---

**5. `UpdateContractExpiresAt` failure — ack not asserted false in old test, now it is**

File: `consumer_test.go`, line 146–170

Old `TestConsumeMobileHandsetOrderedReturnsErrorWhenExpiresAtUpdateFails` only asserted `err != nil`. New Ginkgo version also asserts `Expect(acked).To(BeFalse())`. Stronger — no gap.

---

**6. `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` — `UpdateContractStatus` conflict path untested**

File: `consumer.go`, lines 170–176 (not reflected in any test)

`consumer.go` has a `terrors.ErrConflict` branch inside `UpdateContractStatus` that calls `ack()` and returns nil. This path exists in both the old and the new tests: neither covers it. It's a pre-existing gap but is relevant here because the branch is in the `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` handler that the rewrite purports to fully cover. The new Ginkgo `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` describe block has only two `When` blocks — skipped-if-ordered, and the full happy path — leaving the conflict-resolution branch with zero coverage.

---

**Summary table**

| # | File | ~Line | Summary | Failure scenario |
|---|------|--------|---------|-----------------|
| 1 | `consumer_test.go` | 77 | No test for APPROVED with empty `ContractMonths` and nil `SignedAt` | Future `Atoi("")` call would panic/error with no test catching it |
| 2 | `consumer_test.go` | 46 | Event payload fields `ServiceId/CustomerId/HandsetId` dropped from `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered` helper | Handler change that reads those fields would not be caught by tests |
| 3 | `consumer_test.go` | 208 | `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` skip path only tests `HANDSET_ORDERED`, not `APPROVED` status | Second clause of the `||` guard (added this branch) is entirely untested |
| 4 | `consumer.go` | 170–176 | `ErrConflict` in `UpdateContractStatus` calls `ack()` — no test in old or new file | Silent ack on DB conflict could mask partial-write bugs; no regression protection |

Now I have everything I need. Here are the findings:

---

**Bug 1 — `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts`: double `nowFunc()` call creates a timestamp inconsistency**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go`, lines 400 and 405

The filter is built with one call to `s.nowFunc()` and the `$set` update is built with a second independent call. If `nowFunc` is `time.Now` (as wired in `cmd/handset/jobs.go`), these two calls return slightly different instants. The filter uses T1; `updatedAt` is set to T2 where T2 > T1. This is cosmetically wrong — the stored `updatedAt` is after the cutoff used for matching. Not a correctness bug in isolation, but if anything downstream treats `updatedAt` as "the time we decided it was expired", it will be slightly off. The real fix is to capture `now := s.nowFunc()` once before building both maps.

**Bug 2 — `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts`: infinite loop if MongoDB keeps returning the same document**

File: `storage.go`, lines 411-421

`FindOneAndUpdate` with `SetReturnDocument(options.After)` returns the document **after** the update — i.e., with `status = EXPIRED`. The filter requires `status = HANDSET_ORDERED`. So once updated, that document no longer matches the filter and won't be returned again. The loop is safe.

This is **not a bug** — the termination guarantee holds.

**Bug 3 — Proto field name mismatch: `expired_at` in proto, `expires_at` in generated code**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/protos/mobile/v1/events/service_events.proto`, line 424; generated pb.go line 3390

The proto source says `expired_at = 5` but the generated `.pb.go` has the field tagged as `name=expires_at` and the struct field is `ExpiresAt`. The serialised wire/JSON name will be `expires_at`, not `expired_at`. Any consumer or schema registry that reads the proto source as the contract and expects `expired_at` will be broken. The generated file and the proto source are out of sync — the proto was likely edited after generation without re-running `protoc`. The Go code in `expired_contracts.go` sets `ExpiresAt` which compiles against the generated file, so the Go side works, but the source of truth (the proto) is wrong and will mislead anyone who reads it.

**Bug 4 — `consumer.go`: `expires_at` permanently lost on partial failure (real bug)**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go`, lines 215-238

Sequence:
1. `UpdateContractStatusByID` succeeds → contract is now `HANDSET_ORDERED`.
2. `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails (transient DB error) → function returns a non-nil error, message is nacked and redelivered.
3. On retry: `GetContractByID` returns the contract with `status = HANDSET_ORDERED`.
4. The idempotency check at line 205-209 fires and calls `ack()` immediately — skipping the `UpdateContractExpiresAt` call entirely.

Result: `expires_at` is never written. The expiry job's filter `"expiresAt": {"$lte": now}` will never match this contract (null `expiresAt` doesn't satisfy `$lte`), so it silently never expires.

**Bug 5 — `cmd/handset/jobs.go`: `os.Exit(1)` bypasses deferred cleanup**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/cmd/handset/jobs.go`, lines 87-95

`os.Exit(1)` unwinds the OS process without running deferred functions. The deferred calls in scope are:
- `kafkaSink.Sink.Close()` — buffered async Kafka messages are discarded, potentially losing produced events.
- `protostrateProd.Close()` — same concern; the producer's internal goroutine is abandoned.
- `jobs.Stop()` — the scheduler's `Stop()` calls `c.completion.Wait()`, meaning any in-flight expiry run is not given a chance to finish cleanly.

For the Kafka producer this is the most concrete harm: any events that were batched but not yet flushed to the broker are silently dropped.

**Bug 6 — `jobs.New` captures startup context, not runtime context**

File: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/jobs/jobs.go`, line 29; `scheduler.go`, line 29

`jobs.New(ctx, ...)` is called with the function-local `ctx` from `startJobsMode`. That context is passed into `NewScheduler` and stored as `c.ctx` on the `Scheduler`. The scheduler's `Start` goroutine selects on `c.ctx.Done()` for graceful shutdown — so the shutdown signal has to come from that stored context, not from any externally signalled cancellation unless they are the same context. Looking at `cmd/handset/jobs.go`, the `ctx` passed to `startJobsMode` comes from the bootstrap group's context chain. This is likely fine in practice, but the stored context is the *startup* context, meaning if it is cancelled for an unrelated reason early on (e.g. during init), the scheduler silently exits before jobs have run. The safer pattern is to pass a freshly derived context at `Start()` time or use the errgroup context. Low severity, but worth noting.

---

**Summary ranked by severity:**

| # | File | Approx line | Issue |
|---|------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | `consumer.go` | 205-238 | `expires_at` permanently lost if `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails after status update — real data loss |
| 2 | `cmd/handset/jobs.go` | 93 | `os.Exit(1)` skips deferred Kafka flush and `jobs.Stop()` — in-flight events dropped |
| 3 | `service_events.proto` | 424 | `expired_at` in source vs `expires_at` in generated code — proto source is wrong, will mislead consumers |
| 4 | `storage.go` | 400, 405 | Double `nowFunc()` call makes filter time and `updatedAt` slightly different instants |
| 5 | `jobs.go` | 29 | Scheduler stores startup context; cancellation at wrong time silently stops all jobs |

input.description: Verify: ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired not implemented
input.prompt:
Verify this candidate bug. Return exactly one of CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED, then a one-sentence explanation.

**Candidate:** The generated `service_events.ps.go` adds `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` to the `ConsumerService` interface and registers a Kafka handler for it in `RegisterServiceeventsServiceWithConsumer`. The `consumer.Consumer` struct in `services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go` embeds `serviceevents.UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` which provides a default that just calls `ack()`. If the consumer mode subscribes to the topic that carries `MobileHandsetContractExpired` events, those events will be silently acked without processing.

The question is: **does this matter?** The handset service *produces* these events (via the jobs mode), not consumes them. The no-op default is probably intentional.

To verify:
1. Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go` to see what topics it subscribes to and whether `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` is explicitly implemented or left as the unimplemented stub.
2. Check if the `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` embedding is standard for services that only need a subset of consumers — grep for `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` in other handset service files.

REFUTE if: the no-op default is the standard pattern and the consumer mode clearly does not subscribe to the expiry event topic (the handler registration in `RegisterServiceeventsServiceWithConsumer` only fires if the consumer *subscribes* to that topic, and the registration call is selective).

CONFIRM if: there is explicit handler registration that would consume this event type but no real implementation to handle it.

input.prompt:

Any buffered Kafka messages not yet flushed would be lost.

Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/cmd/handset/jobs.go` lines 70-96 to confirm the structure:

- `defer protostrateProd.Close()` is set before `os.Exit(1)` is called
- `os.Exit(1)` is inside a non-main function (`startJobsMode`) called from `main`

Go's `os.Exit` does NOT run deferred functions even in the calling function's stack. This is a factual Go language behavior.

REFUTE if the defers are in a different scope or if the pattern is otherwise safe.

input.description: Verify: double nowFunc() call in FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts
input.prompt:
Verify this candidate bug. Return exactly one of CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED, then a one-sentence explanation.

**Candidate:** In `services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go`, `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts` calls `s.nowFunc()` twice — once in the filter `"expiresAt": {"$lte": s.nowFunc()}` and once in the update `"updatedAt": s.nowFunc()`. If `nowFunc` is `time.Now`, these return different time instants T1 and T2 (T2 > T1). More importantly, each loop iteration computes a new `s.nowFunc()` for both the filter and the update. A contract with `expiresAt = T1+epsilon` (expiring just after the first call) could be matched in an early iteration but missed in a later one if the filter time didn't advance enough — or more precisely, the filter threshold advances each iteration, which means contracts that expired AFTER the job started could be picked up mid-run.

Read lines 396-425 of `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go` to confirm.

The specific concern is:
1. Is `nowFunc()` called fresh on each loop iteration (not captured once before the loop)?
2. Does this mean the filter boundary shifts mid-run (picking up newly-expired contracts during the same job run)?

CONFIRM if `nowFunc()` is called inside the loop (boundary shifts each iteration).
PLAUSIBLE if called before the loop but still called twice (cosmetic timestamp inconsistency).
REFUTE if the behavior is obviously intentional or harmless.

lastPrompt: go scrutinise the changes

content.text:
CONFIRMED. The `filter` and `update` variables (lines 398-407) are defined **outside the loop** and **evaluated once** at function entry. However, `s.nowFunc()` is called **twice at function entry** (lines 400 and 405), not inside the loop. The actual concern about the boundary shifting mid-run is **not present** — the same filter threshold is reused for every iteration.

The real issue is simpler: the filter and update timestamps are captured once but represent two different instants (T1 vs T2), so contracts updated have `updatedAt = T2` but matched by `expiresAt <= T1`, creating a minor clock-skew inconsistency. The loop itself does not cause the boundary to shift mid-run.

**Verdict: REFUTED** — The filter boundary is fixed for the entire loop; `nowFunc()` is not re-evaluated per iteration, so newly-expired contracts cannot be picked up mid-run.

The question is: **does this matter?** The handset service *produces* these events (via the jobs mode), not consumes them. The no-op default is probably intentional.

To verify:
1. Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go` to see what topics it subscribes to and whether `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` is explicitly implemented or left as the unimplemented stub.
2. Check if the `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` embedding is standard for services that only need a subset of consumers — grep for `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` in other handset service files.

REFUTE if: the no-op default is the standard pattern and the consumer mode clearly does not subscribe to the expiry event topic (the handler registration in `RegisterServiceeventsServiceWithConsumer` only fires if the consumer *subscribes* to that topic, and the registration call is selective).

CONFIRM if: there is explicit handler registration that would consume this event type but no real implementation to handle it.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me verify my understanding:

1. In `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go`, the `Consumer` struct embeds `serviceevents.UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` (line 44).

2. The Consumer implements explicit handlers for `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` (line 58) and `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered` (line 188), but NOT for `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired`.

3. Looking at the generated code in `service_events.ps.go`, `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` has a default implementation for `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` at lines 261-263 that just returns `ack()` without processing.

4. In `RegisterServiceeventsServiceWithConsumer` (lines 853-1095), there is indeed a registration entry for `MobileHandsetContractExpired` at lines 1089-1094 that registers the handler `_ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired`.

5. However, looking at `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/cmd/handset/consumer.go`, the consumer mode in handset service does NOT call `RegisterServiceeventsServiceWithConsumer`. Instead, it only calls `tpubsub.InitProtostrateMobileConsumerWithDeadletter` with the consumer instance, which does NOT automatically register all handlers.

The key insight: the `InitProtostrateMobileConsumerWithDeadletter` function (based on the pattern in the codebase) uses the consumer struct's implemented methods directly. Since `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` is not explicitly implemented in the handset service's Consumer, it will use the unimplemented stub from the embedding, which silently acks.

This means the candidate bug claim is **correct in its observation** but the critical question is whether this matters. Since the handset consumer does not subscribe to or process these events (the service only produces them), having the no-op default is not actually a bug—it's intentional that the expiry event is produced but not consumed by the handset service.

REFUTED

The no-op default for `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractExpired` is the standard pattern in this codebase where services embed `UnimplementedServiceeventsConsumer` to selectively implement only the event consumers they need, and the handset service clearly does not subscribe to or process contract expiry events (it only produces them), making the unimplemented stub appropriate.

content.text:
My bad. Here's what I found:

**1. `expires_at` permanently lost on retry** — `consumer.go:205`
If `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails after `UpdateContractStatusByID` already succeeded, the message is nacked and redelivered. On retry, the contract is already `HANDSET_ORDERED` so the early-exit guard fires and acks — `UpdateContractExpiresAt` is never retried. The contract silently never expires.

**2. Proto field name mismatch** — `service_events.proto:423`
Proto defines field 5 as `expired_at` but the generated Go struct encodes it as `expires_at` on the wire. The proto source and wire format are out of sync — looks like one was edited without regenerating the other.

**3. `os.Exit(1)` skips deferred cleanup** — `jobs.go:94`
When `StartBootstrapGroup` returns an error, `os.Exit(1)` is called inside `startJobsMode` where `defer kafkaSink.Sink.Close()` and `defer protostrateProd.Close()` are registered. `os.Exit` doesn't run defers — buffered Kafka messages get dropped.

**4. Double `nowFunc()` in `FindAndUpdateExpiredContracts`** — `storage.go:398`
The filter and the `updatedAt` in the update are built from two separate `nowFunc()` calls, so `updatedAt` ends up slightly after the expiry cutoff used to find the document. Minor, but inconsistent.

**5. `UpdateContractStatus` ErrConflict branch untested** — `consumer_test.go:208`
The `ConsumeMobileHandsetContractApproved` handler has a branch that silently acks on `ErrConflict`. Neither old nor new tests cover it.

Finding 1 is the most important — it's a silent data-loss bug.

content.text:
The problem is this sequence:

1. Status updated to `HANDSET_ORDERED` ✓
2. `UpdateContractExpiresAt` fails → message nacked, will retry
3. On retry: contract is already `HANDSET_ORDERED` → early exit, `ack()` immediately
4. `expires_at` never gets set

What should happen on step 3? Two options:

**Option A** — in the `HANDSET_ORDERED` early-exit block, also check if `expires_at` is nil and set it before acking.

**Option B** — restructure so `expires_at` is written *before* the status update, so a retry on the status update also retries the expiry write.

Which feels right to you?

## Report: SignedAt Field Writing and UpdateContractStatusByID Usage

### Storage Functions That Write `signedAt` to MongoDB

There is **one storage function** that writes `signedAt` to MongoDB:

   - Location: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go:309-342`
   - Writes `signedAt` conditionally: `setFields["signedAt"] = outcome.SignedAt` (line 324)
   - This function receives an `EligibilityOutcome` which contains the `SignedAt` field
   - Updates the contract status to `ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED` and sets multiple fields based on the outcome

### Full Body of `UpdateContractStatusByID`

**Function Location:** `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go:265-268`

```go
func (s storage) UpdateContractStatusByID(ctx context.Context, contractID uuid.UUID, status models.ContractStatus) error {
	filter := bson.M{"id": contractID}
	return s.updateContractStatus(ctx, filter, status, expectedCurrentStatuses(status)...)
}
```

This is a simple wrapper that delegates to the internal `updateContractStatus` method. The actual update logic is in the `updateContractStatus` helper function (lines 283-307), which:
- Builds a filter based on the contract ID and expected current statuses
- Updates only the `status` and `updatedAt` fields
- Returns error if no documents matched

### Contract Model Fields

**Location:** `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models/models.go:54-82`

Relevant fields in the `Contract` struct:
- `SignedAt *time.Time` (line 71) - stored as `signedAt` in MongoDB with `omitempty` tag
- `ContractMonths string` (line 63) - stored as `contractMonths` in MongoDB

### All Callers of `UpdateContractStatusByID`

There are **4 callers** of `UpdateContractStatusByID`:

1. **Real Implementation Call (consumer.go)**
   - Location: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go:215`
   - Function: `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered` (line 188-241)
   - Context: When a `MobileHandsetOrdered` event is consumed, it updates the contract status to `HANDSET_ORDERED`
   - Code: `if err := c.store.UpdateContractStatusByID(ctx, contractID, models.ContractStatusHandsetOrdered);`

2. **Mock Definition (consumer mocks)**
   - Location: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/mocks/consumer.go:107`
   - Used in tests

3. **Mock Recorder (consumer mocks)**
   - Location: `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/consumer/mocks/consumer.go:115`
   - Used in test expectations

4. **Test Cases (consumer_test.go)**
   - Multiple test calls at lines 89, 112, 138, 160 expecting `UpdateContractStatusByID` to be called with `ContractStatusHandsetOrdered`
   - Tests verify behavior when signing documents and calculating expiry dates

### Key Relationships

- **SignedAt Population**: The `signedAt` field comes from the eligibility provider callback via `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` in service.go (line 297), which calls `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`
- **ContractMonths Usage**: When `UpdateContractStatusByID` transitions a contract to `HANDSET_ORDERED`, the consumer then uses both `SignedAt` and `ContractMonths` to calculate `ExpiresAt` by calling `UpdateContractExpiresAt` (line 235 in consumer.go)

input.content:
# Context

`expires_at` was being calculated in `consumer.go` after `UpdateContractStatusByID` succeeded. If `UpdateContractExpiresAt` then failed, the message was nacked but on retry the `HANDSET_ORDERED` early-exit guard fired — meaning `expires_at` was permanently never written and the contract would never be expired by the job.

Fix: move `expires_at` calculation into the storage layer, computed atomically alongside `signed_at` in the same `$set` operation in `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`. This way `expires_at` is always present on any contract that has a `signed_at`.

## Approach

### 1. `models/models.go` — add `ContractMonths` to `EligibilityOutcome`

```go
type EligibilityOutcome struct {
    Decision           string
    AgreementID        string
    DocusignEnvelopeID string
    ContractMonths     string   // add this
    SignedAt           *time.Time
    CompletedAt        *time.Time
}
```

### 2. `service/service.go` — populate `ContractMonths` when building the outcome

At the `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` call site (line ~297), the `contract` is already fetched. Add `ContractMonths: contract.ContractMonths` to the `EligibilityOutcome` struct literal passed to `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`.

### 3. `storage/storage.go` — compute `expiresAt` atomically in `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`

Inside the `if outcome.SignedAt != nil` block, also compute and set `expiresAt` when `ContractMonths` parses successfully:

```go
if outcome.SignedAt != nil {
    setFields["signedAt"] = outcome.SignedAt
    if outcome.ContractMonths != "" {
        if months, err := strconv.Atoi(outcome.ContractMonths); err == nil {
            setFields["expiresAt"] = outcome.SignedAt.AddDate(0, months, 0)
        }
    }
}
```

Delete `UpdateContractExpiresAt` entirely from `storage.go`.

### 4. `consumer/consumer.go` — remove `expires_at` logic and interface method

- Remove `UpdateContractExpiresAt` from the `Storage` interface.
- Remove the entire `if contract.SignedAt != nil { ... UpdateContractExpiresAt ... }` block from `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered`.

### 5. `consumer/mocks/consumer.go` — remove `UpdateContractExpiresAt` mock

Delete the `UpdateContractExpiresAt` and `UpdateContractExpiresAt` recorder methods.

## Files touched

- `services/handset/internal/models/models.go`
- `services/handset/internal/service/service.go`
- `services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go`
- `services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go`
- `services/handset/internal/consumer/mocks/consumer.go`

## Tests to update

- `storage/storage_test.go` — add a case to the `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved` describe block asserting that `expiresAt` is included in the `$set` when `signedAt` + `ContractMonths` are provided; add a case where `SignedAt` is nil and assert `expiresAt` is not set.
- `consumer/consumer_test.go` — remove all `store.EXPECT().UpdateContractExpiresAt(...)` expectations; remove any tests that only existed to cover that path (the expiry-at-update tests are now covered in the storage layer tests).

## Verification

```
cd services/handset && go test ./...
```

input.summary: Move `expires_at` calculation into the storage layer, computed atomically alongside `signed_at` in `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`. This removes the separate `UpdateContractExpiresAt` call from the consumer entirely, eliminating the retry gap bug. Touches 5 source files + their tests.
input.plan:
# Context

`expires_at` was being calculated in `consumer.go` after `UpdateContractStatusByID` succeeded. If `UpdateContractExpiresAt` then failed, the message was nacked but on retry the `HANDSET_ORDERED` early-exit guard fired — meaning `expires_at` was permanently never written and the contract would never be expired by the job.

Fix: move `expires_at` calculation into the storage layer, computed atomically alongside `signed_at` in the same `$set` operation in `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`. This way `expires_at` is always present on any contract that has a `signed_at`.

## Approach

### 1. `models/models.go` — add `ContractMonths` to `EligibilityOutcome`

```go
type EligibilityOutcome struct {
    Decision           string
    AgreementID        string
    DocusignEnvelopeID string
    ContractMonths     string   // add this
    SignedAt           *time.Time
    CompletedAt        *time.Time
}
```

### 2. `service/service.go` — populate `ContractMonths` when building the outcome

At the `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` call site (line ~297), the `contract` is already fetched. Add `ContractMonths: contract.ContractMonths` to the `EligibilityOutcome` struct literal passed to `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`.

### 3. `storage/storage.go` — compute `expiresAt` atomically in `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`

Inside the `if outcome.SignedAt != nil` block, also compute and set `expiresAt` when `ContractMonths` parses successfully:

```go
if outcome.SignedAt != nil {
    setFields["signedAt"] = outcome.SignedAt
    if outcome.ContractMonths != "" {
        if months, err := strconv.Atoi(outcome.ContractMonths); err == nil {
            setFields["expiresAt"] = outcome.SignedAt.AddDate(0, months, 0)
        }
    }
}
```

Delete `UpdateContractExpiresAt` entirely from `storage.go`.

### 4. `consumer/consumer.go` — remove `expires_at` logic and interface method

- Remove `UpdateContractExpiresAt` from the `Storage` interface.
- Remove the entire `if contract.SignedAt != nil { ... UpdateContractExpiresAt ... }` block from `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered`.

### 5. `consumer/mocks/consumer.go` — remove `UpdateContractExpiresAt` mock

Delete the `UpdateContractExpiresAt` and `UpdateContractExpiresAt` recorder methods.

## Files touched

- `services/handset/internal/models/models.go`
- `services/handset/internal/service/service.go`
- `services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go`
- `services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go`
- `services/handset/internal/consumer/mocks/consumer.go`

## Tests to update

- `consumer/consumer_test.go` — remove all `store.EXPECT().UpdateContractExpiresAt(...)` expectations; remove any tests that only existed to cover that path (the expiry-at-update tests are now covered in the storage layer tests).

## Verification

```
cd services/handset && go test ./...
```

file.content:
package models

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

type ContractStatus string

const (
	ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
	ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
	ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
	ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
	ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
	ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
)

type Handset struct {
	ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
	BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
	Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
	QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
	CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
	Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
}

type HandsetProperties struct {
	Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
	Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
	RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
	RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
	ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
	Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
	Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
	Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
	Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
	ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
	Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
	SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
	WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
	ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
	DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
	SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
	IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
	UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
	PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
	EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
	SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
	BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
}

type Contract struct {
	ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
	ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
	CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
	HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
	HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
	HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
	ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
	RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
	ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
	RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
	LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
	BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
	BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
	Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
	AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
	DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
	SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
	// This field is calculated when handset is ordered
	ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
	CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
	FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
	FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
	FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
	FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
	Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
	CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
	UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
}

type EligibilityOutcome struct {
	Decision           string
	AgreementID        string
	DocusignEnvelopeID string
	SignedAt           *time.Time
	CompletedAt        *time.Time
}

type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
	FailureReason  string
	FailureStage   string
	FailureMessage string
	FailedAt       *time.Time
}

	"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/handsetdb"

)

//go:generate mockgen -destination=./mocks/$GOFILE -source=./$GOFILE

type MongoCollection interface {
	InsertOne(ctx context.Context, document any, opts ...*options.InsertOneOptions) (*mongo.InsertOneResult, error)
	UpdateOne(ctx context.Context, filter any, update any, opts ...*options.UpdateOptions) (*mongo.UpdateResult, error)
	FindOne(ctx context.Context, filter any, opts ...*options.FindOneOptions) *mongo.SingleResult
	FindOneAndUpdate(ctx context.Context, filter any, update any, opts ...*options.FindOneAndUpdateOptions) *mongo.SingleResult
	Find(ctx context.Context, filter any, opts ...*options.FindOptions) (cur *mongo.Cursor, err error)
}

package models

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

type ContractStatus string

const (
	ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
	ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
	ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
	ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
	ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
	ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
)

type Handset struct {
	ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
	BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
	Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
	QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
	CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
	Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
}

type HandsetProperties struct {
	Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
	Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
	RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
	RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
	ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
	Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
	Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
	Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
	Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
	ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
	Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
	SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
	WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
	ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
	DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
	SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
	IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
	UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
	PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
	EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
	SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
	BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
}

type Contract struct {
	ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
	ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
	CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
	HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
	HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`

	ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`

	LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
	BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
	BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`

	AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`

	FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
	FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
	Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
	CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
	UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
}

type EligibilityOutcome struct {
	Decision           string
	AgreementID        string
	DocusignEnvelopeID string
	SignedAt           *time.Time
	CompletedAt        *time.Time
}

type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
	FailureReason  string
	FailureStage   string
	FailureMessage string

}

package models

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

type ContractStatus string

const (
	ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
	ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
	ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
	ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
	ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
	ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
)

type Handset struct {
	ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
	BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
	Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
	QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
	CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
	Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
}

type HandsetProperties struct {
	Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
	Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
	RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
	RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
	ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
	Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
	Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
	Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
	Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
	ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
	Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
	SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
	WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
	ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
	DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
	SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
	IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
	UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
	PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
	EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
	SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
	BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
}

type Contract struct {
	ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
	ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
	CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
	HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
	HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
	HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
	ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
	RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
	ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
	RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
	LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
	BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
	BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
	Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
	AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
	DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
	SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
	ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
	CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
	FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
	FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
	FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
	FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
	Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
	CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
	UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
}

type EligibilityOutcome struct {
	Decision           string
	AgreementID        string
	DocusignEnvelopeID string
	ContractMonths     string
	SignedAt           *time.Time
	CompletedAt        *time.Time
}

type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
	FailureReason  string
	FailureStage   string
	FailureMessage string
	FailedAt       *time.Time
}

message.content: explain the service.go changes

content.text:
In `service.go`, `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` receives an `EligibilityOutcome` as a parameter (built by the caller). The contract is fetched from the DB at line 284. We added one line before the storage call:

```go
outcome.ContractMonths = contract.ContractMonths
```

`outcome` is a value type (struct), so this just mutates the local copy. It injects the `ContractMonths` from the already-fetched contract into the outcome before passing it down to storage, where the `expiresAt` calculation now lives.

That's the entire change — one line, no new DB calls, no new logic.

message.content: i dont get it did we not already have the contract months in?

content.text:
The `EligibilityOutcome` struct didn't have a `ContractMonths` field before our change — we added it. So the caller building the outcome had no way to include it even if they wanted to.

But more importantly: `ContractMonths` isn't something the eligibility provider sends back. It's already stored on the contract in MongoDB from when the contract was created. So the storage layer couldn't compute `expiresAt` without it being passed in explicitly.

That's why we:
1. Added `ContractMonths` to `EligibilityOutcome`
2. Populated it from the contract we'd already fetched in `EligibilityCallbackSuccess`
3. Let storage use it to compute `expiresAt` atomically

message.content: so at what point do we get "signedAt" and "contractMonths" in?

content.text:
`signedAt` comes from the eligibility provider's callback — it's in the `EligibilityOutcome` the caller passes into `EligibilityCallbackSuccess`. It's the date the customer signed the credit agreement.

`contractMonths` is stored on the contract when it's first created — set from the handset's `ContractTermMonths` in `service.go:239`:
```go
ContractMonths: fmt.Sprintf("%d", props.ContractTermMonths)
```

So by the time `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` runs, both values are available — `signedAt` arrives in the callback payload, `contractMonths` is already in the DB.

message.content: so why did we need to add the extra contract monthjs?

content.text:
Because `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved` in storage only receives the `EligibilityOutcome` struct. Before our change that struct had no `ContractMonths` field, so storage had no way to compute `expiresAt` — it only knew `signedAt`.

We needed to thread `ContractMonths` through from the contract (already in DB) into the outcome struct so storage could do `signedAt + contractMonths` in one atomic write.

content.text:
Yes it can — via aggregation pipeline updates (MongoDB 4.2+). You'd replace the `$set` doc with a pipeline that references the existing `contractMonths` field:

```js
[{ $set: { expiresAt: { $dateAdd: { startDate: "$signedAt", unit: "month", amount: { $toInt: "$contractMonths" } } } } }]
```

That would make the whole thing self-contained in storage with no need to pass `ContractMonths` through the outcome at all.

Worth switching to that approach?

package models

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

type ContractStatus string

const (
	ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
	ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
	ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
	ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
	ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
	ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
)

type Handset struct {
	ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
	BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
	Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
	QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
	CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
	Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
}

type HandsetProperties struct {
	Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
	Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
	RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
	RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
	ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
	Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
	Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
	Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
	Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
	ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
	Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
	SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
	WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
	ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
	DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
	SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
	IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
	UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
	PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
	EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
	SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
	BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
}

type Contract struct {
	ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
	ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
	CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
	HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
	HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
	HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
	ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
	RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
	ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
	RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
	LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
	BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
	BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
	Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
	AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
	DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
	SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
	// This field is calculated when eligibility is approved
	ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
	CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
	FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
	FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
	FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
	FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
	Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
	CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
	UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
}

type EligibilityOutcome struct {
	Decision           string
	AgreementID        string
	DocusignEnvelopeID string
	ContractMonths     string
	SignedAt           *time.Time
	CompletedAt        *time.Time
}

type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
	FailureReason  string
	FailureStage   string
	FailureMessage string
	FailedAt       *time.Time
}

content.text:
A few issues:

**1. Duplicate `if outcome.SignedAt != nil` in storage.go**

We check it once to build `setFields`, then again to choose the update type. The second check is only needed because of the first. Could be one block instead of two.

**2. `$toInt: "$contractMonths"` — null propagation**

If `contractMonths` is missing or null on a document, `$toInt(null)` returns null, and `$dateAdd` with `amount: null` sets `expiresAt` to null. Silently wrong rather than an error. Low risk in practice since `contractMonths` is always written at contract creation, but worth knowing.

**3. Storage test only asserts the key exists, not the expression**

```go
Expect(setFields).To(HaveKey("expiresAt"))
```

This doesn't verify the actual `$dateAdd` / `$toInt` structure. A future change that replaced it with a wrong literal value would pass the test. Could be tightened.

**4. `consumer_test.go` Ginkgo conversion** — this was already in progress before our changes (the `consumer_suite_test.go` was pre-existing as untracked). Our changes are consistent with that direction.

Nothing is broken, but items 1 and 3 are easy cleanup. Want to fix those?

message.content: so we get singed at when elegibility is approved?

content.text:
Yes — `signedAt` comes in as part of the eligibility callback payload (`EligibilityOutcome.SignedAt`). It's the timestamp from the eligibility provider (Duologi presumably) indicating when the customer signed the credit agreement.

So the sequence is:
1. Customer signs credit agreement with eligibility provider
2. Provider sends a callback to our service
3. We handle it in `EligibilityCallbackSuccess` → `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`
4. That's where `signedAt` (and now `expiresAt`) gets written atomically

message.content: ok good thats all im concerned with then. are all tests paths covered regarding our changes?

content.text:
For our specific changes:

**Storage `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved`**:
- `signedAt` set → pipeline update with correct `$dateAdd` expression ✓
- `signedAt` nil → regular `$set` without `expiresAt` ✓

**Consumer `ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered`**:
- Already `HANDSET_ORDERED` → skips and acks ✓
- Not `APPROVED` → errors without acking ✓
- `APPROVED` → updates status and acks ✓

The only pre-existing gap (not introduced by us) is `UpdateContractStatusByID` returning `ErrConflict` in the consumer — that path calls `ack()` silently and has no test, but we didn't touch it.

Coverage for our changes is complete.

No. The storage test verifies the `$dateAdd` expression is correctly formed, but we never assert what value `expiresAt` actually ends up as in a document. That's a limitation of mocking — we can't verify the MongoDB arithmetic result.

That gap is acceptable here since the `$dateAdd` / `$toInt` logic is MongoDB's responsibility, not ours. What we can and do test is that the right expression is sent to the driver.

index 60e6b06a..02a75634 100644
--- a/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go
+++ b/services/handset/internal/consumer/consumer.go
@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@ import (
 	"math"
 	"strconv"
 	"strings"
+	"time"
 
 	"github.com/google/uuid"
 	"github.com/utilitywarehouse/protoc-gen-protostrate/protostrate/message"
@@ -27,6 +28,7 @@ type Storage interface {
 	UpdateContractStatus(ctx context.Context, requestID string, status models.ContractStatus) error
 	UpdateContractStatusByID(ctx context.Context, contractID uuid.UUID, status models.ContractStatus) error
 	UpdateContractLoanReference(ctx context.Context, requestID string, loanReference string) error
+	UpdateContractExpiresAt(ctx context.Context, contractID uuid.UUID, expiresAt time.Time) error
 }
 
 type AccountClient interface {
@@ -224,5 +226,16 @@ func (c *Consumer) ConsumeMobileHandsetOrdered(ctx context.Context, env *message
 	tlog.WithAttribute("contract_id", contractIDStr).
 		Info(ctx, "contract marked handset ordered")
 
+	if contract.SignedAt != nil {
+		contractMonths, err := strconv.Atoi(contract.ContractMonths)
+		if err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("invalid contract_months %q for contract %s: %w", contract.ContractMonths, contractIDStr, err)
+		}
+		expiresAt := contract.SignedAt.AddDate(0, contractMonths, 0)
+		if err := c.store.UpdateContractExpiresAt(ctx, contractID, expiresAt); err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("failed to set expires_at for contract %s: %w", contractIDStr, err)
+		}
+	}
+

+	}
